name: Maasai Mara National Reserve
description: Famous for the Great Migration and its high concentration of predators.

name: Nairobi National Park
description: The world's only wildlife park within a capital city.

name: Serengeti National Park
description: A massive ecosystem known for the annual migration of over 1.5 million wildebeest.

name: Tsavo National Park
address: Tsavo East & West National Parks, Kenya
description: One of the oldest and largest parks in Kenya, famous for its red elephants.

name: Ruma National Park
location: Lambwe Valley, Homa Bay County, Kenya
description: The only terrestrial park in Kenya where the Roan antelope is found.

name: Fort Jesus Museum
address: P.O. BOX 82412 - 80100 Nkrumah Rd, Mombasa, Kenya
description: A Portuguese fort built in 1593 and a UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: Hell's Gate National Park
description: Named for the intense geothermal activity within its boundaries.

name: Amboseli National Park
description: Offers the best views of Mount Kilimanjaro and large herds of elephants.

name: Tsavo East National Park
description: Famous for the Galana River and the Yatta Plateau.

name: Lake Nakuru National Park
description: Renowned for its large masses of pink flamingos and rhino sanctuary.

name: Mount Kenya National Park
description: Home to the second highest peak in Africa and diverse alpine flora.

name: Diani Beach
description: A major beach resort on the Indian Ocean coast of Kenya.

name: Lamu Old Town
description: The oldest and best-preserved Swahili settlement in East Africa.

name: Samburu National Reserve
description: Known for unique wildlife like the Grevy's zebra and Reticulated giraffe.

name: Lake Naivasha
description: A high-altitude freshwater lake great for bird watching and hippos.
